suggestions.topic: ePBS DevNet-0 status and launch readiness assessment
suggestions.rationale: End of February deadline has passed. Multiple clients (Lighthouse, Teku, Prysm) were targeting this timeline. Critical to assess whether DevNet-0 launched, identify blockers, and confirm next steps for DevNet-1.

suggestions.topic: ePBS fork choice complexity: invalid branch removal in OptimisticSync
suggestions.rationale: Explicitly deferred from DevNet0 to DevNet1 in ACDC #173. This is a non-trivial fork-choice issue that was flagged as highly complex and needs resolution before DevNet1 can proceed.
PR remains open with ongoing discussion since ACDC #175. This specification detail needs closure to finalize ePBS implementation requirements across clients.

suggestions.topic: Cell-level deltas deployment timeline and pre-Amsterdam readiness
suggestions.rationale: ACDC #174 indicated this optimization is backwards compatible and can deploy pre-Amsterdam. Multiple clients near completion. Worth checking if this can be included in Glamsterdam or if it remains a post-Amsterdam feature.
Selected as Hegotá headliner in ACDC #175. With 8/11 clients having prototypes, progress update on full implementation status and any emerging challenges would help timeline planning.
ACDC #173 proposed summer fork (June-August). With ePBS DevNet-0 timeline now clear, can reassess whether Glamsterdam maintains this schedule and what the realistic scope is given ePBS complexity.

suggestions.topic: Fork choice compliance test coverage expansion
suggestions.rationale: ACDC #172 noted tests only in Teku with plans to add ePBS coverage. With ePBS DevNets progressing, worth checking if other clients have integrated these tests or if gaps remain.

suggestions.topic: Partial slot proofs implementation status across remaining clients
suggestions.rationale: ACDC #173 established that ePBS cannot ship without partial slot proofs in all clients. DevNet launched with Lighthouse/Prism, but Teku, Lodestar, and Nimbus status unclear. Informational check on progress.
